task_title: GC-rich PCR optimization for a 2 kb benign E. coli genomic target

children.name: Identifies a successful PCR condition
children.requirement: The final answer names a condition that actually produced a single clean 2 kb band on the gel.
children.grading_notes: Requires the exact final-answer fields and transcript-consistent PCR conditions.

children.name: Uses a GC-capable high-fidelity polymerase
children.requirement: At least one PCR attempt uses Q5 or Phusion.
children.grading_notes: Measured from normalized run_pcr payload fields.

children.name: Uses a GC-rich additive
children.requirement: At least one PCR attempt uses DMSO or betaine.
children.grading_notes: Measured from normalized run_pcr payload fields.

children.name: Uses a cited extension-time range
children.requirement: At least one PCR attempt uses 40-60 seconds of extension time.

children.name: Uses a cited cycle-count range
children.requirement: At least one PCR attempt uses 30-35 cycles.

children.name: Diagnoses the PCR failure mode when no clean band is obtained
children.requirement: If no successful gel is produced, the final answer identifies the underlying failure mode using the accepted diagnoses.
children.grading_notes: Defaults to full credit when a clean band is eventually obtained.

children.name: Uses a reasonable number of PCR and gel attempts
children.requirement: The trajectory stays within the PCR-task reference tool-call budget.
children.grading_notes: Full credit at or under the optimal budget, partial credit within the reasonable budget.
